_protocol: Calibration topic: Federal Reserve target rate at end of 2026. Prospective estimation from April 2026 baseline.

meta.title: Fed Funds Rate by End 2026
meta.question: Where will the Federal Reserve target rate be on December 31, 2026?
meta.resolution: FOMC official target range as announced at or following the December 2026 FOMC meeting (scheduled Dec 9-10, 2026). Resolved against the FOMC statement on federalreserve.gov.

tagConfig._docs: Tag configuration for monetary policy topic. HAWKISH tags favor H1/H4 (no cut or hike). DOVISH tags favor H2/H3 (cuts). DATA tags are neutral until values are known.

H1.label: No further cuts — stays 3.50-3.75%
H1.description: Fed holds all year. Tariff-driven inflation keeps CPI sticky above 3%, forcing Powell to maintain the pause indefinitely. Dot plot median signals one cut but FOMC ultimately does not deliver it. Consistent with stagflation-lite scenario where labor market softens but inflation prevents easing.

H2.label: One cut — 3.25-3.50%
H2.description: Dot plot median outcome. Fed delivers one 25bp cut, likely in September or December. Inflation moderates gradually, unemployment ticks up to ~4.8-5.0%, but Fed moves cautiously. Most consistent with current guidance and SEP projections.

H3.label: Two+ cuts — 3.00-3.25% or below
H3.description: Labor market deteriorates faster than expected (unemployment 5.2%+), tariff shock proves disinflationary via demand destruction, or financial conditions tighten sharply. Goldman and Bankrate both see 2-3 cuts as plausible. Requires either clear disinflation signal or meaningful growth scare.

H4.label: Rate hike — inflation forces reversal
H4.description: Tariff pass-through re-accelerates CPI to 4%+, inflation expectations become unanchored, or a dollar crisis forces the Fed's hand. Hike of 25-50bp. Historically rare once an easing cycle begins, but tariff environment creates genuine tail risk. H4 is not negligible given current policy uncertainty.

posteriorHistory.note: Initial priors. Anchored on dot plot median (one cut), adjusted up for H3 given Goldman/Bankrate forecasts, assigned meaningful H4 tail for tariff inflation risk. Unemployment at 4.6% creates genuine two-way risk.
Reacceleration of CPI to 3.3% + single-cut median dot + consecutive holds all compatible with H1 (no cuts) or H2 (one cut); sharply lowers H3 (two+ cuts); modestly raises H4 (hike) tail risk.

redTeam.challenge: No significant shifts proposed; no challenge needed.

inflation_regime.label: Inflation Regime
inflation_regime.description: Current CPI/PCE trajectory and whether it is consistent with further easing
inflation_regime.note: Core PCE ~2.8% as of March 2026. Tariff pass-through adds upside risk. Not falling fast enough to unlock cuts confidently, not re-accelerating to hike territory yet.

posteriorEffect.FALLING_TO_TARGET: Boosts H2/H3 significantly. H3 +10-15pp if sustained.
posteriorEffect.RE-ACCELERATING: Boosts H1/H4. H4 +10pp if CPI breaks 4%.

labor_market.label: Labor Market Condition
labor_market.note: Unemployment 4.6%. Payrolls positive but decelerating. Consistent with gradual normalization, not yet a recession signal. Tariff uncertainty suppressing business investment.

posteriorEffect.RECESSION_SIGNAL: Emergency cut scenario. H3 dominant, H4 collapses.

market_pricing.label: Fed Funds Futures Implied Cuts
market_pricing.description: Number of 25bp cuts priced in by Dec 2026 futures
market_pricing.note: Approximate — futures mid-point between one and two cuts as of early April. Represents market consensus, which has historically over-anticipated cuts in the 2024-2026 cycle.
market_pricing.posteriorEffect: Futures pricing >2.5 cuts would boost H3 +10pp. Pricing <0.5 cuts would collapse H3 and boost H1.

fomc_communication.label: FOMC Communication Posture
fomc_communication.description: Directional lean in most recent FOMC statement and press conference
fomc_communication.note: March FOMC held steady, Powell maintained 'wait and see' on tariffs. Neither signaling imminent cut nor hike. Dot plot still projects one cut median.

_design_rules: Each indicator must be OBSERVABLE — actions, not opinions
Each must have a clear fired/not-fired threshold
Every hypothesis needs at least one pro AND one anti indicator
Governor flags unfalsifiable hypotheses as DEGRADED

tier1_critical.desc: FOMC announces a 25bp rate cut at any meeting. Observable in FOMC statement on federalreserve.gov same day.
tier1_critical.posteriorEffect: First cut: H2 +20pp, H1 -20pp. Second cut within same year: H3 +20pp, H2 -15pp.

tier1_critical.desc: FOMC announces a 25bp+ rate hike at any meeting. Observable in FOMC statement.
Month-over-month core CPI prints +0.5% or higher for two consecutive months, or year-over-year core CPI crosses 4.0%. BLS release, verifiable.
BLS monthly unemployment rate reaches 5.5% or higher in any single report.
tier1_critical.posteriorEffect: H3 +25pp. Emergency cut scenario becomes likely. H1/H4 both -10pp.

tier1_critical.desc: June or September SEP dot plot median moves to zero cuts in 2026 (from current one-cut median). Observable in published SEP table.
June or September SEP dot plot median moves to two or more cuts in 2026. Observable in published SEP table.

tier2_strong.desc: Core PCE falls to 2.5% or below on a 3-month annualized basis. BEA release, verifiable.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H2/H3 combined +10pp. Removes key obstacle to cuts.

tier2_strong.desc: December 2026 Fed funds futures consistently price in 2+ cuts (>50bp) for 10+ consecutive trading days.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H3 +8pp, H2 -5pp. Market is a noisy but influential signal.

tier2_strong.desc: Non-farm payrolls print below +50,000 for two consecutive months. BLS release.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H3 +10pp, H1 -5pp. Labor weakening accelerates cut timeline.

tier2_strong.desc: Powell or FOMC majority explicitly signals imminent cut in press conference or congressional testimony — not just 'data dependent' but conditional language pointing to specific meeting.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H2 +10pp. Note: rhetoric only, must fire before T1 cut confirmation.

tier2_strong.desc: Fed Beige Book or regional Fed surveys show widespread, broad-based tariff price pass-through reported by 70%+ of districts. Observable in published Beige Book.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H1 +8pp, H4 +5pp. Signals inflation stickier than base case.

tier2_strong.desc: 2yr-10yr spread inverts beyond -50bp for 30+ consecutive days. Observable via Treasury data / FRED.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H3 +8pp. Recession signal historically precedes emergency cuts.

tier2_strong.desc: BEA advance GDP estimate shows negative growth in any quarter of 2026 (Q1 or Q2 advance release).
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H3 +15pp. Recession forces hand on cuts regardless of inflation.

tier3_suggestive.desc: Published FOMC minutes show majority of participants discussed easing in the near term. Observable in minutes 3 weeks post-meeting.
Unemployment reaches 4.8% in any monthly BLS report.
Three consecutive monthly CPI reports show core m/m of 0.2% or below.
Fed Vice Chair or multiple governors give speeches emphasizing downside labor risks (not inflation). Observable in published speech transcripts.
Goldman Sachs revises its 2026 Fed cut forecast upward to 3+ cuts in a published research note.
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H3 +3pp. Wall Street forecasters move together; one leading revision often precedes consensus shift.

tier3_suggestive.desc: White House announces material rollback or delay of tariffs affecting >20% of import volume. Observable in official trade policy announcements.
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H3 +5pp, H4 -5pp. Removes key inflation upside risk.

tier3_suggestive.desc: 5yr breakeven inflation rate (TIPS spread) rises above 3.0% on FRED for 20+ consecutive trading days.
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H4 +4pp, H3 -4pp. Market pricing in persistent above-target inflation.

anti_indicators.desc: Fed delivers any cut OR dot plot shifts to 2+ cuts for 2026 — directly falsifies no-cut scenario.
Fed holds all year through December AND delivers zero cuts (H1 confirmed), OR delivers two or more cuts (H3 confirmed).
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: H2 collapses to near-zero if either H1 or H3 is confirmed.

anti_indicators.desc: CPI re-accelerates above 4% YoY OR FOMC explicitly signals hike bias — removes room for two-cut scenario.
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: H3 -20pp if inflation regime flips to RE-ACCELERATING.

anti_indicators.desc: Core PCE falls sustainably below 2.5% OR unemployment exceeds 5.5% — removes inflation pressure that would force a hike.
anti_indicators.lr_migration_warning: design prior drift >30% — review before next firing

actorModel.description: The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) is the sole decision-making body. 12 voting members: 7 Fed governors (permanent voters) + 5 rotating regional presidents. Chair Powell dominates public communication and sets agenda framing. Key tension in 2026: tariff-driven inflation vs. softening labor market creates genuine two-way risk, making FOMC unusually data-dependent.

powell.name: Jerome Powell (Chair)
powell.role: FOMC Chair. Controls public narrative, press conference framing, and meeting agenda. Swing vote in contentious decisions.
powell.decisionStyle: Institutionalist. Highly data-dependent. Consensus-builder. Reluctant to surprise markets. Prioritizes Fed credibility over speed.
biases: Anchoring to dot plot: once median is published, needs strong justification to deviate
Asymmetric caution on inflation: scarred by 2021-2022 'transitory' episode, will err hawkish when uncertain
Labor market tolerance: comfortable with unemployment 4.5-5.0% range before treating it as emergency
filters: Congressional testimony requires consistent messaging — cannot pivot sharply between testimonies
Press conference commitments: language used in press conferences binds subsequent communication
Dual mandate: must balance both inflation and employment, cannot optimize for one alone
overrides: Financial stability shock (credit crunch, bank failures) — historical override for emergency cuts
Unemployment spike above 5.5% — would override inflation caution
Explicit White House pressure tends to produce the opposite of intended effect (Powell independence signaling)

fomc_hawks.name: FOMC Hawks (Bowman, Waller, regional presidents)
fomc_hawks.role: Voting members who lean toward maintaining restrictive policy. Constrain Powell's ability to cut without consensus.
fomc_hawks.decisionStyle: Rules-based. Prefer systematic frameworks over discretionary judgment. Resistant to cutting before inflation clearly at 2%.
biases: Overly weight current inflation prints vs. forward-looking signals
Skeptical of yield curve / financial conditions as leading indicators
filters: Dissents are rare — institutional norm to support Chair unless strongly opposed
Speech calendars create public commitment traps
overrides: Unemployment above 5.5% would bring hawks into cut consensus

fomc_doves.name: FOMC Doves (Cook, Jefferson, regional presidents)
fomc_doves.role: Voting members who lean toward easing policy to support employment. Provide vote cushion for cuts when inflation cooperates.
fomc_doves.decisionStyle: Balance-of-risks oriented. Weight employment side of dual mandate more heavily than hawks.
biases: Can be swayed by financial conditions tightening narrative

treasury.name: U.S. Treasury (Bessent)
treasury.role: Does not set rates but influences the environment: debt issuance volume, dollar policy, fiscal stimulus signals. In 2026, tariff revenue policy creates inflation feedback.
treasury.decisionStyle: Fiscal focus. In current administration: willing to accept short-term market volatility for structural goals.
biases: Incentivized to maintain lower rates for debt service cost management
Tariff policy creates inflation that Fed must react to — indirect constraint on rate path
filters: Jawboning by Treasury Secretary historically has limited effect on Fed decisions

markets.name: Fed Funds Futures Market
markets.role: Aggregates private sector expectations. Fed monitors this for communication calibration. Systematic over-pricing of cuts is a documented bias (2024-2026 cycle).
markets.decisionStyle: Bayesian aggregation of analyst views, fast-moving on new data.
biases: Historically over-anticipates cuts in restrictive environments (2024 had 6-7 cuts priced in Jan, delivered only 1)
Subject to narrative momentum — can price in aggressive cuts on single data points

methodology: ACTIONS OVER RHETORIC — FOMC statement language is observable action. Powell speeches are rhetoric until confirmed by vote.
TAG EVERYTHING — Distinguish FOMC votes (EVENT), SEP/dot plot releases (DATA), and Fed speeches (RHETORIC).
DON'T FRONT-RUN — Market pricing of cuts has been wrong in this cycle. Set tripwires and let FOMC votes confirm.
SOCIALIZATION DETECTION — Watch for coordinated dovish/hawkish messaging across multiple Fed officials in the weeks before a meeting. This is the Fed pre-committing.
DOT PLOT ANCHORING — The dot plot median is a strong prior but not a commitment. Count participant distributions, not just medians.

evidenceLog.text: FOMC holds target rate steady at 3.50-3.75% at March 2026 meeting. Statement reiterates 'data dependent' approach. Powell press conference emphasizes tariff uncertainty on both inflation and growth outlooks.
March 2026 SEP dot plot: median projection remains one 25bp cut in 2026 (to 3.25-3.50%). Range of participant views spans zero to three cuts. No participant projects a hike.
evidenceLog.source: Federal Reserve Summary of Economic Projections, March 2026

evidenceLog.text: March 2026 BLS employment report: unemployment 4.6%, non-farm payrolls +145,000. Labor market softening gradually but not deteriorating. Wages +3.8% YoY.

Goldman Sachs forecast (as of April 2026): projects two 25bp cuts in 2026, most likely in September and December, bringing rate to 3.00-3.25%. Cites tariff-driven demand weakness outweighing inflation concerns over second half.
evidenceLog.source: Goldman Sachs Global Investment Research, April 2026
evidenceLog.weightNote: Wall Street forecast, not primary source. Informative for market prior but RHETORIC-adjacent.

evidenceLog.text: Bankrate analyst consensus (April 2026): projects three 25bp cuts in 2026, bringing rate to 2.75-3.00%. Minority view in professional forecaster community.
evidenceLog.weightNote: Retail-facing forecast aggregator. Lower weight than primary Fed or primary research sources.

evidenceLog.text: FOMC minutes from March 17-18 meeting released. Fed officials 'still saw rate cuts as a possibility' but stressed need to look through Iran war energy shock. Minutes show tension between inflation persistence and growth concerns. Rate hike probability rose to 14% before Iran ceasefire.
evidenceLog.source: Federal Reserve FOMC Minutes, released April 8 2026
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: Confirms H2 as modal: cuts still on table but highly conditional. H4 (hike) probability briefly spiked during Iran conflict but has since retreated. No posterior movement — consistent with existing priors.
evidenceLog.note: Primary source (Federal Reserve). US News, Kraken blog both confirm same reading.

evidenceLog.text: March 2026 CPI: headline +0.9% m/m (3.3% YoY) driven by energy +10.9% (Iran war gasoline +21.2%). Core CPI +0.2% m/m (2.6% YoY) — both 0.1pp BELOW consensus. Services ex-energy +0.2% m/m (3.0% YoY). Shelter +0.3% m/m (3.0% YoY, lowest since Aug 2021). Food unchanged. Real earnings -0.6% m/m.
evidenceLog.source: BLS CPI release via CNBC, April 10 2026
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: Mixed signal with H2/H3 lean. Headline inflation alarming (3.3%) but Fed will look through energy. Core below forecast is dovish signal — supports rate cut thesis. Goldman: 'Fed has room to be patient.' Key: shelter at 3.0% is lowest in 5 years — structural disinflation in stickiest component.
evidenceLog.note: BLS primary data. Core CPI below forecast is the critical signal. Tariff visible in apparel (+1%), airlines (+2.7%) but small magnitude.

evidenceLog.text: Goldman Sachs Asset Management (Wilson-Elizondo): 'We believe the Fed will look through the energy-driven noise so long as these factors hold. The Fed has room to be patient, and every reason to do so. Today's number buys the Fed time, but the real test lies ahead.'
evidenceLog.source: Goldman Sachs Asset Management via CNBC, April 10 2026
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — ANALYSIS tag. Informative for market consensus but does not move posteriors. Confirms 'patient' framing consistent with H1 (no cut) or slow H2.
evidenceLog.note: Goldman is a market participant, not a Fed source. Tagged ANALYSIS per framework rules.

evidenceLog.text: FOMC March meeting minutes released April 8 show more Federal Reserve officials now see possible rate hikes in 2026, driven by Iran-war oil shock and tariff-driven inflation persistence. Fed held at 3.50-3.75% for 2nd consecutive meeting. Median dot plot still signals 1 cut but distribution widening.
evidenceLog.source: Federal Reserve Board / US News / Axios
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. H4-directional (hike scenario). t3_fomc_minutes_dovish requires dovish language - these minutes were hawkish, opposite direction. t1_dot_plot_shift_hawkish requires SEP dot plot to shift to zero cuts - median still shows 1 cut. No indicator fired.
evidenceLog.note: Multiple officials discussing hikes is a notable shift. Next FOMC May 6-7 with new SEP.

evidenceLog.text: Treasury Secretary Bessent told Semafor the Fed should 'wait and see' before lowering rates amid Iran war. 'Do I think rates should be lowered? Eventually. I think now that we have to wait and see.' Said Fed is 'doing the right thing by sitting and watching.' On 4%+ growth target for 2026: 'Obviously, we're going to have some make-up to do.' Comments come as Trump continues pushing for rate cuts.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. RHETORIC tag — Bessent is Treasury Secretary, not FOMC. Statement aligns with existing Fed posture (hold). No indicator fires. H1-directional (supports no-cut thesis) but RHETORIC from non-Fed official does not move posteriors per governor rules. Notable: Bessent now publicly at odds with Trump's rate-cut demands.
evidenceLog.note: CNN effectiveTrust 0.7462. Direct quote from Bessent via Semafor. RHETORIC weight = 0.0 per governor. Bessent's 'make-up to do' on growth acknowledges war damage — informative for recession topic cross-reference.

core_pce_yoy.note: Fed's preferred inflation gauge. Target is 2.0%. Current 2.8% is above target but below crisis level.

two_ten_spread.note: Slightly positive. Not inverted. Recession signal not currently active.

breakeven_5yr.note: Market-implied inflation expectations. Above 2.0% target but not alarming. Watch for move above 3.0%.

watchpoints.desc: May FOMC meeting (May 6-7): Will statement language shift? Any dissents? Any hint of June cut possibility?
April CPI release (mid-April): First post-tariff-implementation inflation read. Core CPI above 0.4% m/m would be significant hawkish signal.
Q1 2026 GDP advance estimate (late April): Negative print would materially shift H3 upward.
June FOMC + SEP (Jun 17-18): Next dot plot release. Will median shift? This is the highest-information meeting between now and year-end.
Watch for coordinated Fed governor speeches (~2 weeks before each FOMC meeting) signaling meeting outcome.
CME FedWatch tool: if Dec 2026 futures price in 3+ cuts (implied rate <3.00%) for 10+ consecutive days, trigger t2 indicator review.
White House / Trump statements on Fed independence: aggressive rate-cut demands could trigger Powell credibility defense (hawkish response). Monitor Truth Social and official statements.
